Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low serum protein and globulin), mild liver enzyme elevation (alkaline phosphatase), and abnormal urine findings (protein, glucose, ketones, low specific gravity, brown color). These results suggest possible chronic kidney disease with nephrotic-range proteinuria and possible underlying metabolic or hepatic dysfunction. Clinical correlation and further nephrological and hepatic evaluation are recommended.
